newsSince: Ibiden hit an all-time high on June 17, 2026 (+31% since June 9). The fundamental drivers (FY record results, ~70-80% AI-server substrate share, ¥500B capex / 2.5x capacity plan, Nvidia ties) were disclosed pre-June 9. Newer items are sell-side: Bernstein upgraded to Outperform and Morgan Stanley to Overweight on Nvidia substrate growth -- sentiment/flow, not fresh fundamentals.

sources.label: Ibiden FY operating income / earnings alert (Smartkarma)
Ibiden to invest ¥500B in IC package substrate capacity (Globe and Mail)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the residual-alpha-vs-passives lens, a +31% melt-up to an ATH on no new fundamental evidence (just sell-side upgrades) on top of +240% YTD and a $46B cap further compresses hidden/under-owned upside. Crowding penalty deepens beyond the June 9 down call.
verdicts.bull: Best-in-class AI substrate franchise; analyst upgrades signal more institutional demand.
verdicts.neutral: Story is fully discovered; upgrades chase price, not new fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: +240% YTD, $46B cap, ATH -- essentially zero residual/under-owned alpha left on a crowding-sensitive list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued AI-server substrate capacity ramp into 2027.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the master bottleneck list, the substrate scarcity is real and high-quality, but the news-free +31% pop to an ATH extends rerating without new fundamental evidence beyond known capex/share. Bottleneck-vs-rerating balance pushes residual score modestly lower again.
verdicts.bull: Dominant high-end AI substrate share (~70-80%); record FY results; capacity 2.5x by FY28.
verdicts.neutral: Premier asset but priced for it after +240% YTD; only sell-side news since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Valuation extended; substrate supply catching up could pressure pricing.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI substrate capacity expansion and Nvidia demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Rerating fatigue; ASP/competition risk as supply expands.
